title: Peruvian-Style Pan-Seared Peruvian Beef Saltado
description: This dish features tender Peruvian beef, onions, tomatoes, and french fries in a spicy and savory sauce, served over rice. It's a classic Peruvian recipe that's sure to please.

ingredients.name: 1 pound beef strips (such as sirloin or ribeye)
ingredients.notes: Slice beef into thin strips and season with salt and pepper.

ingredients.name: 2 cups frozen french fries
ingredients.notes: Thaw french fries according to package instructions.

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the beef and cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the skillet and cook the onion until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the beef broth and bring to a boil, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Add the cooked beef back into the skillet, along with the cumin, paprika, salt, black pepper, and Szechuan peppercorns. Stir to combine.
Add the chopped tomatoes and french fries to the skillet and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and the fries are coated.
Stir in the soy sauce, vinegar, and sugar. Cook for an additional minute, until heated through.
Serve hot over rice, garnished with green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
Cook the rice according to package instructions and set aside.
